title: India's maternal mortality rate drops to 97 per 1 lakh live births — historic low
text: India's Sample Registration System data revealed the maternal mortality rate dropped to 97 per 1 lakh live births, the lowest recorded in independent India's history. The target is to reach 70 per lakh by 2030. Institutional deliveries now stand at 89.4% nationally.

text: The National High Speed Rail Corporation Limited conducted a successful trial run of the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High Speed Rail corridor covering a 50 km stretch between Surat and Navsari at 300 km/h. The full 508 km corridor is expected to be operational in phases from 2026 onwards.

title: India wins Kabaddi World Cup 2025 for eighth consecutive time
text: The Indian men's kabaddi team won the Kabaddi World Cup 2025 in Chennai for the eighth consecutive time, defeating Iran 43-28 in the final. India's women's team also retained the title for the fifth straight time. Total attendance at the tournament exceeded 1 lakh across all matches.

title: Mumbai Metro Line 3 fully operational — Colaba to SEEPZ in 44 minutes
text:
Mumbai Metro Line 3 (Aqua Line) became fully operational, connecting Colaba in South Mumbai to SEEPZ in the eastern suburbs in 44 minutes.

The 33.5 km underground metro has 26 stations and is expected to reduce private vehicle traffic on the Western Express Highway by 15%.

title: India's renewable energy capacity crosses 200 GW milestone — Ministry of Power
text: India's total installed renewable energy capacity crossed 200 gigawatts for the first time, the Ministry of Power announced. Solar power contributed 85 GW while wind contributed 46 GW. India is on track to meet its target of 500 GW renewable capacity by 2030 under the Paris Agreement commitments.
